decks.notes: This deck focuses on using low-cost and efficient creatures and burn spells to deal quick, direct damage to the opponent. The strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with early aggressive creatures like Goblin Bushwhacker and Clockwork Percussionist, while also using burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Blast to finish them off. The deck also includes cards that allow for card draw and additional damage, such as Reckless Impulse and Fireblast. With a focus on speed and aggression, this deck aims to quickly deplete the opponent's life total before they have a chance to stabilize.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. It aims to control the board with cards like Contaminated Aquifer and Snuff Out while also efficiently drawing cards and digging for key pieces with spells like Brainstorm and Thought Scour. Gurmag Angler provides a powerful threat to close out the game, supported by efficient removal like Cast Down. The deck's strategy is to maintain control of the game through disruption and efficient card advantage until it can secure a win condition.

The deck focuses on utilizing cheap, efficient spells and creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By combining card draw, counterspells, and graveyard manipulation, the deck can maintain a steady stream of threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan. With a focus on tempo and aggression, the Mono Blue Aggro deck aims to control the game early on and close out the match before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. By utilizing efficient spells like Counterspell and Skred, as well as card advantage engines like Brainstorm and Preordain, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining a steady stream of resources. Additionally, the inclusion of creatures like Azure Fleet Admiral and Murmuring Mystic provides a win condition that can close out the game once the opponent has been sufficiently controlled.

Fast-paced and creature-heavy deck utilizing elves to overwhelm opponents with a swarm of attackers. The deck focuses on generating mana quickly with various elf creatures and has a card advantage engine with Lead the Stampede and Winding Way. Key cards like Timberwatch Elf and Nyxborn Hydra provide power and evasion, while Wellwisher offers a way to gain life and stabilize against aggressive decks. Efficiently utilizing mana and maximizing the elf synergy is crucial to maintaining pressure and closing out games swiftly.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. The key strategy is to generate artifacts as quickly as possible using cards like Ichor Wellspring and Mistvault Bridge, allowing for the efficient deployment of powerful creatures like Myr Enforcer. Galvanic Blast and Krark-Clan Shaman provide removal and additional reach, while Thoughtcast provides card draw and consistency. The deck aims to maintain a fast tempo and apply constant pressure on the opponent to secure a quick victory.

The main idea of this Mono Blue Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost, evasive creatures like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent while disrupting their game plan with counterspells and bounce spells like Sleep of the Dead. The deck aims to maintain card advantage through cantrips like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Thought Scour, while also utilizing card draw spells like Deep Analysis. By efficiently utilizing resources and applying pressure early in the game, this deck seeks to outpace and outmaneuver opponents in a Pauper 1 vs 1 format.

The main focus of this Red Deck Wins pauper deck is to quickly and aggressively overwhelm the opponent with low-cost creatures and burn spells. By utilizing efficient creatures like Goblin Bushwhacker and Burning-Tree Emissary, along with direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Blast, the deck aims to deal quick and consistent damage to the opponent. With minimal mana curve and a focus on speed and aggression, this deck looks to finish the game before the opponent has a chance to establish their own strategy.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing gates for mana fixing and defensive creatures, while also using removal spells and combat tricks to keep the opponent at bay. The deck focuses on gaining incremental advantage through efficient creatures like Gatecreeper Vine and Guardian of the Guildpact, while also utilizing cards like Basilisk Gate and Citadel Gate for additional value. The inclusion of Armadillo Cloak and Malevolent Rumble provides additional reach and threat to the deck. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and overwhelm them with its versatile control strategy.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of removal spells and sacrifice effects to maintain board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck utilizes Golgari (black/green) and Jund (black/green/red) colors to take advantage of graveyard interactions and creature sacrifice. It has a strong emphasis on generating value through card draw with cards like Ichor Wellspring and Lembas, while also utilizing tokens from Khalni Garden and Troll of Khazad-dûm to pressure the opponent. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent through attrition and resource management.

This Jund Aggro deck focuses on quick and efficient creature removal, card advantage, and resource denial. By utilizing spells like Cast Down and Krark-Clan Shaman, along with artifacts such as Nihil Spellbomb and Drossforge Bridge, it aims to control the board while maintaining a steady stream of threats. With Cleansing Wildfire and Twisted Landscape for mana fixing and Graveyard recursion options like Pulse of Murasa and Refurbished Familiar, this deck is built to disrupt the opponent's game plan and close out games with its aggressive creatures.

The main focus of this Mono Blue Terror control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through counterspells and bounce effects while setting up a powerful win condition with cards like Cryptic Serpent and Tolarian Terror. The deck aims to control the board and tempo of the game through efficient draw spells like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Preordain, while also utilizing cards like Deprive and Dispel to protect its threats. The inclusion of Lórien Revealed and Murmuring Mystic provides additional value and win condition options in the late game.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly generate card advantage and maintain control of the board with cheap counterspells and bounce effects, while applying pressure with efficient creatures like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while efficiently deploying threats to close out the game quickly.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly build up a board of aggressive creatures, using cards like Blight Mamba, Glistener Elf, and Ichorclaw Myr, and buffing them with spells like Might of Old Krosa and Mutagenic Growth. The deck also includes cards like Rancor and Snakeskin Veil to protect your creatures and keep up the pressure on your opponent. Overall, the goal is to overwhelm your opponent with fast, efficient creatures and pump spells to win the game before they have a chance to stabilize.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm your opponent quickly with a fast and aggressive strategy focused on dealing direct damage. Utilizing low-cost creatures like Goblin Bushwhackers and Goblin Tomb Raiders, along with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Chain Lightning, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero. With cards like Burning-Tree Emissary and Rally at the Hornburg allowing for explosive turns, this deck looks to end the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on assembling the Urza's Tron lands to generate large amounts of mana for powerful spells while controlling the opponent's board with cards like Stonehorn Dignitary and Moment's Peace. It also utilizes card draw and recursion through spells like Mulldrifter and Mnemonic Wall, as well as various ways to find key pieces like Crop Rotation and Expedition Map. The deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent by gaining incremental advantages through its control elements and eventually overwhelming them with powerful late-game plays.
